dog.notes: Allometric 132×kg^0.75 for very small/large; 40–60 ml/kg/day for adults.

cat.notes: Cats are more conservative — 40–60 ml/kg/day. Use isotonic crystalloid.

ferret.notes: Prone to hypoglycemia — consider dextrose-supplemented LRS.

rabbit.notes: High obligate water need (≈75–100 ml/kg/day). Warm fluids — hypothermia + GI stasis risk.

guinea-pig.notes: Same as rabbit. Vitamin C supplementation alongside fluids.

chinchilla.notes: Heat sensitive — fluids at room temp 20–25°C, not warm.

hamster.notes: Tiny patient — micro-dosing. Use IO if IV impossible. SC LRS up to 5% body weight per site.

hedgehog.notes: Cephalic / jugular access. Warm to 38°C if hibernation attempted.

sugar-glider.notes: IO via tibia or femur if vascular access fails.

bird.notes: ⚠ Air sac anatomy — avoid overhydration (pulmonary edema risk). Always warm fluids. ⚠ RPS: SC inguinal or IO ulna for delivery.

bird-large.notes: Lower per-kg than small birds. IO ulna or distal tibiotarsus access.

reptile.notes: ⚠ ECTOTHERM — half-life longer. Warm to POTZ before/during therapy. Subcoelomic or intracoelomic preferred over SC.

tortoise.notes: Chelonian — 10–25 ml/kg/day. Epicoelomic (prefemoral fossa) preferred. Warm to POTZ.

amphibian.notes: Cutaneous absorption — bath in amphibian Ringer's (or 1/2 LRS) is primary route. Never tap water.
